title: Tetraena mandavillei
taxonomy: Tetraena mandavillei is a plant species classified in the family Martiniaceae. This classification places it within the order Caryophyllales. Further classification details may vary based on the specific taxonomy used.

intro: Tetraena mandavillei, also known as the "Desert Rose" or "Hoodia-like cactus," is a succulent plant species native to the Arabian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used to aid in digestion and reduce appetite, offering potential health benefits in weight management and glucose regulation. The bioactive constituents of Tetraena mandavillei include glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make a decoction, a tincture, or a powder, which can be used to support digestive health and suppress appetite.
